project-join.label: Combine Data from Multiple Tables
project-join.summary: Use JOINs to answer questions across tables.

q4-code-join-products.title: List all products and their supplier names (if available) from the products_catalog dataset.

q5-code-join-customers.title: Find all customers and the names of products they have ordered (if any) from the customers_cleanup dataset.

intro-joins.title: Why Use JOINs?
intro-joins.bodyMarkdown: In real-world databases, information is often split across multiple tables. JOINs let you combine related data, making it possible to answer complex questions that a single table can't handle. For example, you might want to see which customers bought which products, or which students are enrolled in which courses.

types-of-joins.title: Types of JOINs
types-of-joins.bodyMarkdown:
The most common JOINs are:
- **INNER JOIN**: Returns rows where there is a match in both tables.
- **LEFT JOIN**: Returns all rows from the left table, and matched rows from the right table (or NULL if there is no match).
- **RIGHT JOIN**: Returns all rows from the right table, and matched rows from the left table (or NULL if there is no match). (Note: SQLite does not support RIGHT JOIN directly.)
- **FULL OUTER JOIN**: Returns all rows when there is a match in one of the tables. (Not supported in SQLite.)

join-syntax.title: JOIN Syntax
join-syntax.bodyMarkdown:
The basic syntax for an INNER JOIN looks like this:

```sql
SELECT columns
FROM table1
INNER JOIN table2
ON table1.column = table2.column;
```

You can also use LEFT JOIN and other types as needed.

q3-join-multi.title: Select all statements that are true about LEFT JOIN:
q3-join-multi.prompt: Select all statements that are true about LEFT JOIN:

options.a: Returns all rows from the left table
options.b: Returns only matching rows from both tables
options.c: Returns NULLs for non-matching rows from the right table
options.d: Ignores rows from the left table with no match

q4-code-join-products.title: List all products and their supplier names (if available) from the products_catalog dataset.
q4-code-join-products.prompt: Write a SQL query that returns the product name and the supplier name for each product. Show all products, even if they don't have a supplier.

q5-code-join-customers.title: Find all customers and the names of products they have ordered (if any) from the customers_cleanup dataset.
q5-code-join-customers.prompt: Write a SQL query to list each customer's name and the product name for each order. Include customers even if they haven't ordered anything.
